# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TAGS: nutrition, diet, food, Mediterranean, keto, ketogenic, elimination diet, anti-inflammatory diet, AIP, paleo, vegan, vegetarian, carnivore, Gerson, Wahls, low carb, high fat, LCHF, calorie, macros, micronutrients, organic, processed food, sugar, gluten, dairy, lectins, oxalates, histamine, FODMAPs, food sensitivity, food allergy, meal plan, Warburg diet, cancer diet, metabolic diet, eating, autoimmune protocol, fasting, intermittent fasting, glucose, carbs, protein, fat, fiber, cancer nutrition
# CATEGORY: Foundations
# USE FOR: Diet advice, nutrition plans, what to eat, food-related questions, ketogenic for conditions
# COUPLED_SYSTEMS: 12_GUT, 06_METABOLIC, 25_ONCOLOGY_SUPPORT, 38_NUTRITION_PROTOCOLS, 29_WEIGHT_BODY_COMP, 30_FASTING_AUTOPHAGY, 67_GLUTEN_CELIAC_NCGS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# NUTRITION & THERAPEUTIC DIETS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 === SECTION 1: CLAIMS DISCIPLINE [INTERNAL_ONLY] ===
## FOOD IS FOUNDATIONAL

Before any supplement, IV, or advanced therapy — there's food.

**Every day, multiple times a day, you make choices that either:**
- Fuel healing, or
- Feed disease

We're not talking about perfection or restriction for its own sake. We're 
talking about using food strategically as a therapeutic tool.

---

## PRINCIPLES OF THERAPEUTIC NUTRITION

### 1. Real Food First

| Eat More | Eat Less |
|----------|----------|
| Whole, unprocessed foods | Packaged, processed foods |
| Vegetables (lots) | Refined sugars |
| Quality proteins | Industrial seed oils |
| Healthy fats | Artificial ingredients |
| Variety and color | Foods that don't spoil |

**Simple test:** Did this food exist 200 years ago? Could your great-grandmother 
recognize it? If not, it's probably processed.

### 2. Blood Sugar Stability

Unstable blood sugar drives:
- Energy crashes
- Cravings
- Inflammation
- Hormone disruption
- Weight gain
- Mood swings

**Stabilize with:**
- Protein at every meal
- Fiber-rich foods
- Healthy fats
- Avoiding sugar spikes
- Not skipping meals (unless intentional fasting)

### 3. Anti-Inflammatory Eating

Chronic inflammation underlies most diseases. Food can either:

| Pro-Inflammatory | Anti-Inflammatory |
|------------------|-------------------|
| Sugar, refined carbs | Vegetables, especially leafy greens |
| Seed oils (soybean, corn, canola) | Olive oil, avocado, coconut |
| Trans fats | Omega-3 (fatty fish, sardines) |
| Processed meats | Quality proteins |
| Excessive alcohol | Herbs and spices (turmeric, ginger) |
| Foods you're sensitive to | Berries, colorful produce |

### 4. Nutrient Density

Your body needs vitamins, minerals, amino acids, fatty acids. 
**The goal:** Maximum nutrients per calorie.

**Nutrient-dense foods:**
- Liver and organ meats (nature's multivitamin)
- Egg yolks
- Fatty fish (salmon, sardines, mackerel)
- Shellfish (oysters, mussels)
- Leafy greens
- Colorful vegetables
- Bone broth
- Fermented foods

### 5. Gut-Friendly Eating

Your gut microbiome needs:
- **Fiber** (prebiotic — feeds good bacteria)
- **Fermented foods** (probiotic — adds good bacteria)
- **Variety** (different fibers feed different bacteria)
- **Minimal disruptors** (sugar, artificial sweeteners, emulsifiers)

---

## THERAPEUTIC DIETS

Different conditions respond to different dietary approaches. 
Here are the major therapeutic diets we use:

### ELIMINATION DIET

**Purpose:** Identify food sensitivities
**How:** Remove common triggers, then reintroduce systematically

**Phase 1 — Elimination (2-4 weeks):**
Remove:
- Gluten
- Dairy
- Eggs
- Soy
- Corn
- Peanuts
- Sugar
- Alcohol
- Processed foods

**Phase 2 — Reintroduction (1-2 weeks per food):**
- Add one food back for 3 days
- Monitor symptoms for 72 hours
- If reaction, wait until clear, then try next food
- If no reaction, keep in and test next food

**Why it works:** Many symptoms (fatigue, brain fog, joint pain, skin issues, 
digestive problems) are driven by hidden food reactions.

### AIP (AUTOIMMUNE PROTOCOL)

**Purpose:** Calm autoimmune inflammation
**Best for:** Autoimmune diseases (Hashimoto's, RA, IBD, MS, psoriasis, etc.)

**Elimination phase removes:**
- Grains (all)
- Legumes (all)
- Dairy
- Eggs
- Nightshades (tomatoes, peppers, eggplant, potatoes)
- Nuts and seeds
- Sugar and sweeteners
- Alcohol
- Coffee
- Processed foods
- NSAIDs

**Emphasizes:**
- Organ meats
- Bone broth
- Fatty fish
- Vegetables (non-nightshade)
- Fermented foods
- Quality meats
- Healthy fats

**Duration:** 30-90 days strict, then careful reintroduction
**See:** 22_AUTOIMMUNE (Wahls Protocol variation)

### LOW-FODMAP

**Purpose:** Reduce gut fermentation and IBS symptoms
**Best for:** IBS, SIBO, bloating, digestive distress

**FODMAPs are fermentable carbs:**
- **F**ermentable
- **O**ligo-saccharides (fructans, GOS — wheat, onions, garlic, beans)
- **D**isaccharides (lactose — dairy)
- **M**ono-saccharides (fructose — apples, honey, mango)
- **A**nd
- **P**olyols (sugar alcohols — sorbitol, xylitol, stone fruits)

**Phase 1:** Strict low-FODMAP (2-6 weeks)
**Phase 2:** Systematic reintroduction by FODMAP group
**Phase 3:** Personalized diet based on tolerance

**Note:** This is not meant to be long-term. It's a diagnostic and reset tool.

### LOW-HISTAMINE

**Purpose:** Reduce histamine load
**Best for:** Histamine intolerance, MCAS, chronic urticaria, unexplained flushing/itching

**Avoid:**
- Aged foods (cheese, wine, fermented foods, cured meats)
- Fermented foods (ironic — usually healthy but high histamine)
- Alcohol (especially red wine)
- Smoked fish
- Spinach, tomatoes, eggplant, avocado
- Citrus
- Shellfish
- Chocolate
- Leftovers (histamine increases with time)

**Emphasize:**
- Fresh foods (cooked and eaten immediately)
- Fresh meat (not aged)
- Most vegetables (fresh)
- Rice, quinoa
- Fresh fruits (except citrus, strawberries)
- Olive oil, coconut oil

**DAO enzyme** supplements can help when eating out.

**Note:** Address root cause (gut, mast cells) — diet alone is management, not cure.

### KETOGENIC DIET

**Purpose:** Shift to fat-burning, reduce insulin, produce ketones
**Best for:** Metabolic syndrome, obesity, type 2 diabetes, epilepsy, some neurological conditions, some cancers (adjunct)

**Macros (standard keto):**
- Fat: 70-80%
- Protein: 15-20%
- Carbs: 5-10% (<20-50g/day)

**Foods:**
- Fatty meats, fish
- Eggs
- Non-starchy vegetables
- Avocado, olives
- Nuts and seeds
- Coconut, olive oil
- Full-fat dairy (if tolerated)

**Avoid:**
- Grains, bread, pasta
- Sugar, sweets
- Starchy vegetables
- Most fruits (except berries in moderation)
- Legumes

**Variations:**
- Standard ketogenic
- Targeted keto (carbs around workouts)
- Cyclical keto (periodic carb refeeds)
- Carnivore-adjacent

**Caution:** Not for everyone. Monitor lipids. May need electrolyte support.
Some people (APOE4, certain genetics) may not do well on high saturated fat.

### CARNIVORE DIET

**Purpose:** Ultimate elimination diet, reduce plant toxins
**Best for:** Severe autoimmune, refractory gut issues, extreme elimination trial

**What you eat:**
- Meat (beef, lamb, pork, poultry)
- Fish
- Eggs
- Animal fats
- Optionally: Dairy (if tolerated)

**What you avoid:**
- All plants

**Why it can help:**
- Eliminates all plant compounds (oxalates, lectins, phytates, etc.)
- Eliminates fiber (which can irritate some guts)
- Ultimate elimination — nothing left to react to

**Concerns:**
- Long-term sustainability
- Nutrient variety (organ meats help)
- Gut microbiome changes
- Not studied long-term

**Use:** Usually as a reset (30-90 days), then potentially reintroduce foods carefully.

### MEDITERRANEAN DIET

**Purpose:** Anti-inflammatory, cardiovascular, longevity
**Best for:** General health, cardiovascular disease, prevention, APOE4 carriers

**Emphasizes:**
- Olive oil (lots)
- Fish (especially fatty fish)
- Vegetables, fruits
- Legumes
- Nuts
- Whole grains (moderate)
- Herbs and spices
- Moderate red wine (optional)
- Limited red meat
- Limited sugar

**Most studied diet for longevity and cardiovascular health.**

### LOW-OXALATE

**Purpose:** Reduce oxalate load
**Best for:** Kidney stones (calcium oxalate), oxalate sensitivity, some chronic pain

**High-oxalate foods to limit:**
- Spinach (very high)
- Almonds, cashews
- Chocolate
- Beets
- Rhubarb
- Sweet potatoes
- Swiss chard

**Low-oxalate alternatives:**
- Lettuce, cabbage, cauliflower
- Meat, fish, eggs
- Dairy (calcium binds oxalate)
- Macadamia nuts

**Note:** Reduce gradually — rapid reduction can cause "oxalate dumping."

---

## FOOD QUALITY MATTERS

### Animal Products

| Better | Worse |
|--------|-------|
| Grass-fed beef | Feedlot beef |
| Pasture-raised eggs | Conventional eggs |
| Wild-caught fish | Farmed fish (most) |
| Pasture-raised poultry | Conventional poultry |
| Organic dairy | Conventional dairy |

**Why:** Better fatty acid profiles, fewer toxins, more nutrients, no antibiotics/hormones.

### Produce

| Better | Worse |
|--------|-------|
| Organic (especially dirty dozen) | Heavily sprayed |
| Local, seasonal | Shipped from far away |
| Fresh or frozen | Canned with additives |

**Dirty Dozen (prioritize organic):**
Strawberries, spinach, kale, nectarines, apples, grapes, peaches, cherries, 
pears, tomatoes, celery, potatoes

**Clean Fifteen (okay conventional):**
Avocados, corn, pineapple, onions, papaya, peas, asparagus, honeydew, kiwi, 
cabbage, mushrooms, cantaloupe, mangoes, watermelon, sweet potatoes

### Fats and Oils

| Use | Avoid |
|-----|-------|
| Olive oil (cold/low-heat) | Soybean oil |
| Avocado oil (high-heat) | Corn oil |
| Coconut oil | Canola oil |
| Butter/ghee (grass-fed) | Vegetable oil |
| Animal fats (tallow, lard) | Margarine |
| | Trans fats |

**Seed oils are inflammatory** — they're in everything processed. Read labels.

---

## MEAL TIMING

### Time-Restricted Eating (TRE)

Not about what you eat — about when.

**Common patterns:**
- 16:8 (fast 16h, eat in 8h window)
- 14:10 (less restrictive)
- 18:6 (more restrictive)
- OMAD (one meal a day — aggressive)

**Benefits:**
- Autophagy activation
- Insulin sensitivity
- Weight management
- Circadian alignment

**See:** 12_AUTOPHAGY_FASTING for full fasting protocols

### Circadian Eating

- Eat earlier in the day
- Front-load calories
- Light dinner
- Avoid late-night eating
- Align with natural light

---

## PRACTICAL GUIDANCE

### If You're Overwhelmed, Start Here:

1. **Remove:** Sugar, processed foods, seed oils
2. **Add:** More vegetables, quality protein, healthy fats
3. **Stabilize:** Eat regular meals with protein
4. **Hydrate:** Water, not sodas or juice
5. **Observe:** Notice how you feel

### If You Have Specific Conditions:

| Condition | Dietary Approach |
|-----------|------------------|
| Autoimmune | AIP or Wahls |
| IBS/SIBO | Low-FODMAP |
| Histamine issues | Low-histamine |
| Metabolic/Diabetes | Low-carb or ketogenic |
| Inflammation | Anti-inflammatory/Mediterranean |
| Finding triggers | Elimination diet |
| Severe/refractory | Carnivore trial |
| Kidney stones | Low-oxalate |
| Brain/cognitive | Mediterranean, keto, or Bredesen |
| Cancer support | See 25_ONCOLOGY |

---

 === SECTION 2: PATIENT COMMUNICATION [PATIENT_OK] ===
## THERAPEUTIC DIET PROTOCOLS — DETAILED

### AIP Protocol (Full)

**Phase 1: Elimination (30-90 days)**

REMOVE:
```
GRAINS: All grains including rice, oats, wheat, corn, quinoa
LEGUMES: All beans, lentils, peanuts, soy
DAIRY: All dairy products
EGGS: Whole eggs
NIGHTSHADES: Tomatoes, peppers (all), eggplant, potatoes, goji
NUTS & SEEDS: All nuts, all seeds, cocoa, coffee, seed-based spices
ALCOHOL: All alcohol
SWEETENERS: All sugars, artificial sweeteners
FOOD ADDITIVES: Emulsifiers, thickeners, etc.
NSAIDS: Aspirin, ibuprofen, etc.
```

EMPHASIZE:
```
PROTEINS: Grass-fed meat, wild fish, organ meats, bone broth
VEGETABLES: All except nightshades (aim 8-14 cups/day)
FATS: Olive oil, coconut oil, avocado oil, animal fats
FERMENTED FOODS: Sauerkraut, kimchi, kombucha, coconut yogurt
BONE BROTH: Daily
FRUIT: Limited (1-2 servings, lower sugar)
HERBS: Fresh herbs, non-seed spices (turmeric, ginger, etc.)
```

**Phase 2: Reintroduction Protocol**

Order (least likely to react first):
1. Egg yolks (day 1, 3, 5) — whites later
2. Seed-based spices
3. Nuts (one at a time)
4. Seeds (one at a time)
5. Cocoa/chocolate
6. Egg whites
7. Ghee → butter → dairy
8. Coffee
9. Nightshades (one at a time, potatoes first)
10. Alcohol (if desired)
11. Rice → other grains
12. Legumes

**Reintroduction method:**
- Day 1: Small amount
- Day 2: Larger amount
- Day 3: Normal portion
- Days 4-6: Eliminate again and monitor
- If symptoms → keep out longer, retry in 1-3 months
- If clear → incorporate and move to next food

### Wahls Protocol (for MS and Autoimmune)

**Three levels:**

**Wahls Diet (Level 1):**
- 9 cups vegetables daily (3 cups greens, 3 cups sulfur-rich, 3 cups colorful)
- Gluten-free, dairy-free
- Quality meat/fish
- Organ meat weekly

**Wahls Paleo (Level 2):**
- Above plus:
- Remove all grains, legumes
- Remove all processed foods
- Seaweed, fermented foods
- Soaked nuts/seeds only

**Wahls Paleo Plus (Level 3 — Ketogenic):**
- Above plus:
- Ketogenic macros
- 2 cups greens only (carb restricted)
- Coconut oil emphasis
- 12-16 hour daily fast

### Low-FODMAP Detailed

**Phase 1: Restriction (2-6 weeks)**

| FODMAP Group | High Foods to Avoid |
|--------------|---------------------|
| Oligosaccharides (Fructans) | Wheat, rye, onion, garlic, leek |
| Oligosaccharides (GOS) | Legumes, lentils, chickpeas |
| Disaccharides (Lactose) | Milk, yogurt, soft cheese |
| Monosaccharides (Fructose) | Apples, pears, mango, honey, HFCS |
| Polyols | Sorbitol, mannitol, stone fruits, mushrooms |

**Phase 2: Reintroduction**

Test each FODMAP group separately:
- Day 1: Small serving
- Day 2: Medium serving
- Day 3: Large serving
- Days 4-6: Washout period
- Record symptoms

**Phase 3: Personalization**

Based on triggers identified, create long-term sustainable diet avoiding 
personal trigger FODMAPs while including tolerated foods.

### Ketogenic Protocol

**Standard Ketogenic Diet (SKD):**
- 70-75% fat
- 20% protein
- 5-10% carbs (<20-50g net carbs)

**Ketone targets:**
- Nutritional ketosis: 0.5-3.0 mmol/L
- Therapeutic ketosis (epilepsy, cancer): 2-5 mmol/L

**Electrolyte support (critical):**
- Sodium: 3-5g additional
- Potassium: 1-3g additional
- Magnesium: 300-500mg

**Common mistakes:**
- Too much protein (converts to glucose)
- Not enough sodium (keto flu)
- Not enough fat
- Hidden carbs

**Monitoring:**
- Blood ketones (preferred over urine)
- Blood glucose
- Energy, mental clarity
- Lipid panel (some worsen)

**Who should NOT do keto:**
- Pregnant/breastfeeding
- Certain rare metabolic disorders
- Type 1 diabetes (without supervision)
- History of eating disorders (caution)
- Some with gallbladder issues

### Cancer-Specific Nutrition (Adjunctive)

**See 25_ONCOLOGY_SUPPORT for full protocol**

Key principles:
- Metabolic approach (Warburg effect)
- Glucose restriction (cancer feeds on glucose)
- Ketogenic consideration (deprives cancer of preferred fuel)
- Fasting around chemotherapy (may protect normal cells)
- High nutrient density
- Adequate protein (maintain muscle)
- Anti-angiogenic foods (green tea, turmeric, berries)

**Caution:** Always coordinate with oncology team.

### Budwig Protocol

**Traditional Budwig (Dr. Johanna Budwig):**
- Flaxseed oil + cottage cheese (quark) mixture
- Theory: Electron-rich fats restore cellular respiration
- Used historically for cancer

**Preparation:**
- 2 Tbsp flaxseed oil + 4 Tbsp cottage cheese (or quark)
- Blend until oil is completely absorbed (no separation)
- Add fresh fruit, honey, or ground flaxseed
- Eat immediately (oxidizes quickly)

**Note:** Not mainstream, limited controlled evidence, but historically used.

### Gerson Therapy Elements (Overview)

**Note: Gerson is an intensive cancer protocol — partial elements may be used**

Key components:
- Juicing (13 fresh juices daily)
- Coffee enemas (detoxification)
- Organic plant-based diet
- No salt, animal protein (in original)
- Supplements (potassium, B12, thyroid, liver extract)

**If incorporating elements:**
- Increased vegetable juicing (reasonable)
- Coffee enemas (see 34_BIOPHYSICAL)
- Organic emphasis (reasonable)
- Full protocol requires supervision

## FOOD-SUPPLEMENT SYNERGIES

| Nutrient | Best Food Sources | Supplement Form |
|----------|-------------------|-----------------|
| Omega-3 | Fatty fish, sardines | Fish oil, algae oil |
| Vitamin D | Fatty fish, sun | D3 cholecalciferol |
| Magnesium | Dark leafy greens, nuts | Glycinate, malate, threonate |
| Zinc | Oysters, beef, pumpkin seeds | Picolinate, bisglycinate |
| B12 | Liver, meat, fish | Methylcobalamin |
| Iron | Red meat, liver, spinach | Ferrous bisglycinate |
| Selenium | Brazil nuts (2/day), fish | Selenomethionine |
| Vitamin A | Liver, egg yolks | Retinyl palmitate |
| Collagen | Bone broth, skin | Hydrolyzed collagen |
| Probiotics | Fermented foods | Multi-strain supplements |

## FOOD-DRUG INTERACTIONS

| Food | Drug | Interaction |
|------|------|-------------|
| Grapefruit | Many (statins, CCBs, etc.) | Inhibits CYP3A4, increases drug levels |
| Leafy greens (Vitamin K) | Warfarin | Reduces drug effect |
| Tyramine foods | MAOIs | Hypertensive crisis |
| Calcium-rich foods | Thyroid meds, antibiotics | Reduces absorption |
| High-fiber | Some medications | Reduces absorption |
| Alcohol | Metformin, many others | Various interactions |
| Cruciferous (raw excess) | Thyroid meds | May affect iodine uptake |

---

 === SECTION 3: PHARMACOKINETICS & DYNAMICS (ADME) [INTERNAL_ONLY] ===
## DIET SELECTION GUIDE

| Condition | Recommended Diet |
|-----------|------------------|
| Autoimmune | AIP → Wahls |
| IBS/SIBO | Low-FODMAP |
| Histamine intolerance | Low-histamine |
| Metabolic syndrome/T2D | Low-carb/Ketogenic |
| Obesity/Weight loss | Low-carb or Mediterranean |
| Inflammation | Anti-inflammatory/Mediterranean |
| Food sensitivity ID | Elimination |
| Severe/refractory | Carnivore trial |
| Kidney stones (oxalate) | Low-oxalate |
| Cardiovascular | Mediterranean |
| APOE4 carrier | Mediterranean (lower sat fat) |
| Cancer adjunct | Keto/Low-glucose + oncology |
| Brain/Cognitive | Mediterranean, Bredesen |
| General optimization | Whole foods, anti-inflammatory |

## MACROS BY DIET

| Diet | Carbs | Protein | Fat |
|------|-------|---------|-----|
| Standard ketogenic | <10% | 15-20% | 70-75% |
| Low-carb | 10-25% | 25-30% | 45-65% |
| Mediterranean | 40-50% | 15-20% | 30-40% |
| Carnivore | ~0% | 20-35% | 65-80% |
| Standard American (SAD) | 50-60% | 10-15% | 30-35% |

## FOODS TO ALWAYS AVOID

- Trans fats (partially hydrogenated oils)
- High-fructose corn syrup
- Artificial sweeteners (aspartame, sucralose)
- Industrial seed oils in excess
- Highly processed foods
- Artificial colors/preservatives

## NUTRIENT-DENSE PRIORITIES

1. Liver (weekly)
2. Egg yolks (daily)
3. Fatty fish (2-3x/week)
4. Bone broth (daily)
5. Leafy greens (daily)
6. Fermented foods (daily)
7. Colorful vegetables (daily)

## REINTRODUCTION SEQUENCE (GENERAL)

1. Least reactive foods first
2. One food at a time
3. 3-day test period
4. 3-day washout
5. Monitor symptoms 72 hours
6. Clear → keep, React → remove

## HYDRATION BASICS

- Water: 0.5 oz per lb body weight (more if active)
- Add minerals/electrolytes if needed
- Limit: Coffee, alcohol, sugary drinks
- Signs of dehydration: Dark urine, fatigue, headache

---
